543	      tags: [policy]
544	
545	    # Run full test suite before push
546	    full-tests:
547	      name: "Full test suite"
548	      root: "cli/"
549	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
550	      tags: [go, test]
551	
552	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
553	    no-debug:
554	      name: "No debug statements"
555	      run: |
556	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
557	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
558	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
559	          status=$?
560	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
561	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
562	            exit 0
563	          fi
564	          exit 1

data.text: Now let me look at existing hook tests to understand the pattern used, and also the perf test mentioned in the bead.

data.output:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"os/exec"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"strings"
8		"testing"
9	
10		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	func initRepoWithTrackedBeadsFile(t *testing.T) string {
16		t.Helper()
17		dir := t.TempDir()
18		initTestRepo(t, dir)
19		trackerDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
20		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(trackerDir, 0o755))
21		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(trackerDir, "beads.jsonl"), []byte(`{"id":"ddx-test","status":"open"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
22		runGitForBeadsHookTest(t, dir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
23		runGitForBeadsHookTest(t, dir, "commit", "-m", "seed tracker")
24		return dir
25	}
26	
27	func runGitForBeadsHookTest(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) {
28		t.Helper()
29		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
30		cmd.Dir = dir
31		cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
32		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
33		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
34	}
35	
36	func runBeadsTrackerHook(t *testing.T, repoRoot string) (string, error) {
37		t.Helper()
38		script, err := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join("..", "..", "scripts", "beads-tracker-health.sh"))
39		require.NoError(t, err)
40		cmd := exec.Command("sh", script)
41		cmd.Dir = repoRoot
42		cmd.Env = append(gitpkg.CleanEnv(), "DDX_BEADS_TRACKER_HEALTH_ROOT="+repoRoot)
43		out, runErr := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		return string(out), runErr
45	}
46	
47	func appendTrackerLine(t *testing.T, repoRoot, id string) {
48		t.Helper()
49		path := filepath.Join(repoRoot, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
50		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
51		require.NoError(t, err)
52		defer f.Close()
53		_, err = f.WriteString(`{"id":"` + id + `","status":"open"}` + "\n")
54		require.NoError(t, err)
55	}
56	
57	func TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsCleanTracker(t *testing.T) {
58		dir := initRepoWithTrackedBeadsFile(t)
59	
60		out, err := runBeadsTrackerHook(t, dir)
61		require.NoError(t, err, out)
62	}
63	
64	func TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsFullyStagedTrackerChange(t *testing.T) {
65		dir := initRepoWithTrackedBeadsFile(t)
66		appendTrackerLine(t, dir, "ddx-staged")
67		runGitForBeadsHookTest(t, dir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
68	
69		out, err := runBeadsTrackerHook(t, dir)
70		require.NoError(t, err, out)
71	}
72	
73	func TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnUnstagedTrackerChange(t *testing.T) {
74		dir := initRepoWithTrackedBeadsFile(t)
75		appendTrackerLine(t, dir, "ddx-unstaged")
76	
77		out, err := runBeadsTrackerHook(t, dir)
78		require.Error(t, err)
79		assert.Contains(t, out, ".ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged changes")
80		assert.Contains(t, out, "git add .ddx/beads.jsonl")
81	}
82	
83	func TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnPartiallyStagedTrackerChange(t *testing.T) {
84		dir := initRepoWithTrackedBeadsFile(t)
85		appendTrackerLine(t, dir, "ddx-staged")
86		runGitForBeadsHookTest(t, dir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
87		appendTrackerLine(t, dir, "ddx-unstaged")
88	
89		out, err := runBeadsTrackerHook(t, dir)
90		require.Error(t, err)
91		assert.Contains(t, out, ".ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged changes")
92		assert.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "partially staged"), "output should mention partial staging: %s", out)
93		assert.Contains(t, out, "git add .ddx/beads.jsonl")
94	}
95

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"os/exec"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8	
9		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)
13	
14	// initTestRepo creates a git repo at dir with a clean initial commit.
15	func initTestRepo(t *testing.T, dir string) {
16		t.Helper()
17		env := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
18	
19		run := func(args ...string) {
20			t.Helper()
21			c := exec.Command("git", args...)
22			c.Dir = dir
23			c.Env = env
24			out, err := c.CombinedOutput()
25			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
26		}
27		run("init")
28		run("config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
29		run("config", "user.name", "Test")
30		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README"), []byte("x"), 0o644))
31		run("add", "README")
32		run("commit", "-m", "init")
33	}
34	
35	func hasIssueType(issues []DiagnosticIssue, t string) bool {
36		for _, i := range issues {
37			if i.Type == t {
38				return true
39			}
40		}
41		return false
42	}
43	
44	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare verifies that core.bare=true on a repo
45	// with a working tree is detected, and that --fix removes it.
46	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare(t *testing.T) {
47		dir := t.TempDir()
48		initTestRepo(t, dir)
49	
50		// Corrupt the repo — set core.bare=true on a repo that clearly has a work tree.
51		corrupt := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.bare", "true")
52		corrupt.Dir = dir
53		corrupt.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
54		require.NoError(t, corrupt.Run())
55	
56		// Without --fix: detection only.
57		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
58		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_core_bare_corruption"),
59			"expected git_core_bare_corruption issue, got: %+v", issues)
60	
61		// Confirm core.bare still present.
62		getBare := exec.Command("git", "config", "--local", "--get", "core.bare")
63		getBare.Dir = dir
64		getBare.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
65		out, _ := getBare.Output()
66		assert.Contains(t, string(out), "true", "core.bare should still be set before --fix")
67	
68		// With --fix: remediate.
69		issues = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, true)
70		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_core_bare_corruption"),
71			"issue should still be reported under --fix (as fixed)")
72	
73		getBare2 := exec.Command("git", "config", "--local", "--get", "core.bare")
74		getBare2.Dir = dir
75		getBare2.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
76		if err := getBare2.Run(); err == nil {
77			t.Fatalf("core.bare should be unset after --fix")
78		}
79	}
80	
81	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsStrayCoreWorktree verifies stray core.worktree detection.
82	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsStrayCoreWorktree(t *testing.T) {
83		dir := t.TempDir()
84		initTestRepo(t, dir)
85	
86		bogus := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "not-the-real-worktree")
87		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.worktree", bogus)
88		set.Dir = dir
89		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
90		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
91	
92		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
93		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
94			"expected git_stray_core_worktree issue, got: %+v", issues)
95	
96		// --fix should remove it.
97		_ = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, true)
98		get := exec.Command("git", "config", "--local", "--get", "core.worktree")
99		get.Dir = dir
100		get.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
101		if err := get.Run(); err == nil {
102			t.Fatalf("core.worktree should be unset after --fix")
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsPrimaryCheckoutCoreWorktreeRedirect verifies
107	// the incident shape where the primary checkout's .git/config redirects Git
108	// commands to a different worktree path.
109	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsPrimaryCheckoutCoreWorktreeRedirect(t *testing.T) {
110		dir := t.TempDir()
111		initTestRepo(t, dir)
112	
113		redirect := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "redirected-worktree")
114		add := exec.Command("git", "worktree", "add", "--detach", redirect, "HEAD")
115		add.Dir = dir
116		add.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
117		require.NoError(t, add.Run())
118	
119		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.worktree", redirect)
120		set.Dir = dir
121		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
122		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
123	
124		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
125		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
126			"expected git_stray_core_worktree issue, got: %+v", issues)
127	}
128	
129	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthFixUnsetsRedirectedCoreWorktree verifies --fix removes
130	// the redirect and a subsequent health check no longer reports the corruption.
131	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthFixUnsetsRedirectedCoreWorktree(t *testing.T) {
132		dir := t.TempDir()
133		initTestRepo(t, dir)
134	
135		redirect := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "redirected-worktree")
136		add := exec.Command("git", "worktree", "add", "--detach", redirect, "HEAD")
137		add.Dir = dir
138		add.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
139		require.NoError(t, add.Run())
140	
141		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.worktree", redirect)
142		set.Dir = dir
143		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
144		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
145	
146		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, true)
147		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
148			"expected git_stray_core_worktree issue, got: %+v", issues)
149	
150		issues = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
151		assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
152			"core.worktree redirect should be gone after --fix, got: %+v", issues)
153	
154		get := exec.Command("git", "config", "--local", "--get", "core.worktree")
155		get.Dir = dir
156		get.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
157		if err := get.Run(); err == nil {
158			t.Fatalf("core.worktree should be unset after --fix")
159		}
160	}
161	
162	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsLocalHooksPath verifies stale local
163	// core.hooksPath detection and --fix remediation. A local hooksPath causes
164	// lefthook to print "Skipping hook sync" and can leave stale hooks active.
165	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsLocalHooksPath(t *testing.T) {
166		dir := t.TempDir()
167		initTestRepo(t, dir)
168	
169		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.hooksPath", ".git/hooks")
170		set.Dir = dir
171		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
172		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
173	
174		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
175		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_local_hooks_path"),
176			"expected git_local_hooks_path issue, got: %+v", issues)
177	
178		issues = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, true)
179		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_local_hooks_path"),
180			"issue should still be reported under --fix (as fixed)")
181	
182		get := exec.Command("git", "config", "--local", "--get", "core.hooksPath")
183		get.Dir = dir
184		get.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
185		if err := get.Run(); err == nil {
186			t.Fatalf("core.hooksPath should be unset after --fix")
187		}
188	}
189	
190	// TestPreCommitDDXValidateFailsOnCoreWorktreeRedirect verifies the pre-commit
191	// guard used by lefthook fails when local config points the primary checkout
192	// at a different worktree.
193	func TestPreCommitDDXValidateFailsOnCoreWorktreeRedirect(t *testing.T) {
194		dir := t.TempDir()
195		initTestRepo(t, dir)
196	
197		redirect := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "redirected-worktree")
198		add := exec.Command("git", "worktree", "add", "--detach", redirect, "HEAD")
199		add.Dir = dir
200		add.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
201		require.NoError(t, add.Run())
202	
203		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.worktree", redirect)
204		set.Dir = dir
205		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
206		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
207	
208		script, absErr := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join("..", "..", "scripts", "git-config-health.sh"))
209		require.NoError(t, absErr)
210		cmd := exec.Command("sh", script)
211		cmd.Dir = dir
212		cmd.Env = append(gitpkg.CleanEnv(), "DDX_GIT_CONFIG_HEALTH_ROOT="+dir)
213		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
214		require.Error(t, err, "hook guard should fail on redirected core.worktree")
215		assert.Contains(t, string(out), "Invalid local git config: core.worktree=")
216		assert.Contains(t, string(out), "git config --unset core.worktree")
217	
218		lefthook, readErr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join("..", "..", "lefthook.yml"))
219		require.NoError(t, readErr)
220		assert.Contains(t, string(lefthook), "git-config-health:")
221		assert.Contains(t, string(lefthook), "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh")
222	}
223	
224	// TestPreCommitGitConfigHealthFailsOnCoreHooksPath verifies the pre-commit
225	// guard fails before lefthook can silently continue with stale hook-path config.
226	func TestPreCommitGitConfigHealthFailsOnCoreHooksPath(t *testing.T) {
227		dir := t.TempDir()
228		initTestRepo(t, dir)
229	
230		set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.hooksPath", ".git/hooks")
231		set.Dir = dir
232		set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
233		require.NoError(t, set.Run())
234	
235		script, absErr := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join("..", "..", "scripts", "git-config-health.sh"))
236		require.NoError(t, absErr)
237		cmd := exec.Command("sh", script)
238		cmd.Dir = dir
239		cmd.Env = append(gitpkg.CleanEnv(), "DDX_GIT_CONFIG_HEALTH_ROOT="+dir)
240		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
241		require.Error(t, err, "hook guard should fail on local core.hooksPath")
242		assert.Contains(t, string(out), "Invalid local git config: core.hooksPath=.git/hooks")
243		assert.Contains(t, string(out), "git config --unset-all --local core.hooksPath")
244	}
245	
246	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthCleanRepo verifies a clean repo produces no corruption
247	// issues (only the optional worktreeConfig warning at most).
248	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthCleanRepo(t *testing.T) {
249		dir := t.TempDir()
250		initTestRepo(t, dir)
251	
252		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
253	
254		assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_core_bare_corruption"),
255			"clean repo should not report core.bare corruption")
256		assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
257			"clean repo should not report stray core.worktree")
258	}
259	
260	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthWorktreeConfigWarning verifies that a missing
261	// extensions.worktreeConfig surfaces as a (non-fatal) warning issue.
262	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthWorktreeConfigWarning(t *testing.T) {
263		dir := t.TempDir()
264		initTestRepo(t, dir)
265	
266		issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
267		assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_worktree_config_disabled"),
268			"expected git_worktree_config_disabled warning on fresh repo")
269	
270		// Enable it, then the warning should disappear.
271		en := exec.Command("git", "config", "extensions.worktreeConfig", "true")
272		en.Dir = dir
273		en.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
274		require.NoError(t, en.Run())
275	
276		issues = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
277		assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_worktree_config_disabled"),
278			"warning should clear once extensions.worktreeConfig=true")
279	}
280	
281	// TestCheckGitRepoHealthNonGitDir verifies the check is a no-op outside a repo.
282	func TestCheckGitRepoHealthNonGitDir(t *testing.T) {
283		dir := t.TempDir()

285		assert.Nil(t, issues, "non-git dir should return nil issues")

data.text: Now I understand the pattern. Let me look at how to test lefthook.yml YAML parsing in existing tests, and look at the full context of the lefthook.yml around the pre-push section.

445	
446	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
447	    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
448	    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
449	    evidence-lint-all:
450	      <<: *go_checks
451	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
452	      root: "cli/"
453	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'
454	
455	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
456	    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
457	    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
458	    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
459	    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
460	    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
461	    runtime-lint-all:
462	      <<: *go_checks
463	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
464	      root: "cli/"
465	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'
466	
467	    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
468	    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
469	    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
470	    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
471	    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
472	    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
473	    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
474	    # cli/ Go source.
475	    routing-lint-all:
476	      <<: *go_checks
477	      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
478	      root: "cli/"
479	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'
480	
481	    docs-routing-lint:
482	      name: "Docs Routing Lint (legacy DDx routing surfaces allowlisted)"
483	      run: 'bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh'
484	
485	    # YAML validation
486	    yaml-validate:
487	      name: "Validate YAML files"
488	      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
489	      run: |
490	        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
491	          for file in {all_files}; do
492	            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
493	          done
494	        fi
495	
496	# ====================
497	# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
498	# ====================
499	pre-push:
500	  parallel: false
501	
502	  commands:
503	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
504	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
505	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
506	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
507	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
508	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
509	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
510	    merge-policy:
511	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
512	      run: |
513	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
514	        remote="${1:-origin}"
515	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
516	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
517	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
518	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
519	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
520	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
521	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
522	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
523	          exit 0
524	        fi
525	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
526	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
527	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
528	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
529	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
530	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
531	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
532	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
533	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
534	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
535	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
536	          echo ""
537	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
538	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
539	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
540	          exit 1
541	        fi
542	        exit 0
543	      tags: [policy]
544	
545	    # Run full test suite before push
546	    full-tests:
547	      name: "Full test suite"
548	      root: "cli/"
549	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
550	      tags: [go, test]
551	
552	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
553	    no-debug:
554	      name: "No debug statements"
555	      run: |
556	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
557	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
558	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
559	          status=$?

13	// TestCheckVersionGateBlocksOldBinaryAgainstNewerProject is the ddx-9655497c
14	// acceptance test for FEAT-015 AC-007: a project that records a newer
15	// required DDx version in .ddx/versions.yaml must refuse to run against an
16	// older binary, with actionable guidance.
17	func TestCheckVersionGateBlocksOldBinaryAgainstNewerProject(t *testing.T) {
18		workDir := t.TempDir()
19		// Project insists on v0.9.0; binary reports v0.6.0.
20		writeProjectVersion(t, workDir, "0.9.0")
21	
22		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
23		factory.Version = "0.6.0"
24	
25		err := factory.checkVersionGate(&cobra.Command{Use: "list"}) // exempt list isn't one of the recovery names
26		require.Error(t, err)
27		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "0.9.0")
28		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "0.6.0")
29		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ddx upgrade")
30	}
31	
32	// TestCheckVersionGateAllowsNewerBinary covers the common case: the
33	// operator upgraded first. Binary >= project = no block.
34	func TestCheckVersionGateAllowsNewerBinary(t *testing.T) {
35		workDir := t.TempDir()
36		writeProjectVersion(t, workDir, "0.6.0")
37	
38		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
39		factory.Version = "0.9.0"
40	
41		require.NoError(t, factory.checkVersionGate(&cobra.Command{Use: "list"}))
42	}
43	
44	// TestCheckVersionGateSkipsWhenNoVersionsFile covers a fresh project: no
45	// .ddx/versions.yaml, no gate.
46	func TestCheckVersionGateSkipsWhenNoVersionsFile(t *testing.T) {
47		workDir := t.TempDir()
48		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
49		factory.Version = "0.6.0"
50		require.NoError(t, factory.checkVersionGate(&cobra.Command{Use: "list"}))
51	}
52	
53	// TestCheckVersionGateBypassedForDevBinary keeps dev builds unblocked so
54	// DDx engineers never trip the gate on in-flight work.
55	func TestCheckVersionGateBypassedForDevBinary(t *testing.T) {
56		workDir := t.TempDir()
57		writeProjectVersion(t, workDir, "0.9.0")

data.text:
Now I have all the context I need. Let me make the changes:

1. Fix `lefthook.yml` to set `CGO_ENABLED=1` and add an actionable error path for missing C compilers
2. Add the two required tests

input.old_string:
# Run full test suite before push
    full-tests:
      name: "Full test suite"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
      tags: [go, test]
input.new_string:
# Run full test suite before push
    full-tests:
      name: "Full test suite"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        # Race detector requires cgo. Explicit CGO_ENABLED=1 overrides any
        # inherited CGO_ENABLED=0 in the shell environment.
        export CGO_ENABLED=1
        if ! (command -v gcc >/dev/null 2>&1 || command -v cc >/dev/null 2>&1 || command -v clang >/dev/null 2>&1); then
          echo "ERROR: go test -race requires CGO_ENABLED=1 and a C compiler (gcc/cc/clang)."
          echo "Install a C compiler (e.g. apt install gcc or brew install gcc) to use the race-detector gate."
          exit 1
        fi
        go test -race -cover ./...
      tags: [go, test]
